steps.title: Identify what the speaker wants from the ocean
steps.content: The speaker pleads: "Rock me to sleep, ye waves" and asks for a "soothing slumberous lullaby." The imagery throughout—drifting "far out from toil and care," gentle winds, a peaceful sea and sky—points mainly to a desire for calm and rest.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice and find the match
steps.content:
Consider every option:
  • Choice A — ‘To illustrate the increasing intensity of the speaker's desire to escape ongoing hardship by gliding on the ocean’: The poem briefly mentions leaving "toil and care," but it does not build in intensity or make ongoing hardship the focus; the emphasis is on the tranquility sought. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘To contrast the demands of the speaker's everyday life with the serenity of being rocked to sleep by the ocean’: The poem points to burdens through "toil and care," but it does not develop a contrast with everyday demands; it is almost entirely devoted to describing the peaceful state desired. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘To convey the speaker's longing for the ocean to impart a sense of inner tranquility’: The entire poem is a plea for the ocean’s gentle rocking and sound to bring the speaker peace and rest—inner tranquility is exactly the goal. ✓
  • Choice D — ‘To justify the speaker's qualms about being transported by the ocean to a quiet destination’: The speaker has no hesitation or qualms; the tone is one of longing and invitation, not doubt. ✗
That confirms C is correct.
